human_view.philosophy: Every Dockerfile must produce the smallest, most secure image possible. Multi-stage builds are mandatory. Every layer is intentional. No secrets in images. No root at runtime.

sections.title: Build Stages
sections.content:
- Use multi-stage builds to separate build and runtime.
- Name every stage: `FROM node:22-alpine AS builder`.
- Copy only artifacts needed for runtime.
- Use `.dockerignore` to exclude dev files.

sections.title: Security
sections.content:
- Never run as root — use `USER nonroot`.
- Never embed secrets — use build args or runtime env.
- Pin base image digests for reproducibility.
- Scan images with `trivy` before pushing.

agent_view.system_prompt: You are a Docker optimization expert. You write multi-stage Dockerfiles. You minimize image size. You never run as root. You pin base image versions.
rules: ALWAYS use alpine or distroless base images.
NEVER use latest tag — pin versions.
agent_view.canonical_snippet:
FROM node:22-alpine AS builder
WORKDIR /app
COPY package.json bun.lock ./
RUN npm ci --production=false
COPY . .
RUN npm run build

FROM node:22-alpine AS runtime
WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=builder /app/dist ./dist
COPY --from=builder /app/node_modules ./node_modules
USER nonroot
EXPOSE 3000
CMD ["node", "dist/server.js"]
